sections.title: Tasks
sections.body: Tasks are things you do. They live on your Today, This Week, and Beyond tabs based on when they're due.

subsections.title: Recurring Tasks
subsections.body: Set a task to repeat daily, on specific weekdays, monthly, or annually. Tether automatically advances the due date each time you complete it.

subsections.title: One-Time Tasks
subsections.body: Due once. Swipe right to complete and dismiss it.

subsections.title: Chore Lists
subsections.body: A checklist-style task with sub-items. Great for room-by-room cleaning, shared household responsibilities, or anything with multiple steps. Tap All Done to reset and recur.

subsections.title: Grocery Lists
subsections.body: A smart shopping list with a built-in catalog. Items are grouped by department. Add custom items, adjust quantities, and mark items as you shop.

sections.title: Events
sections.body: Events are things you attend. Create an event, invite people, and coordinate who's bringing what.

subsections.title: Attendees & RSVP
subsections.body: Invite household members or contacts. Guests can RSVP Going, Tentative, or Can't make it. You always see who's coming.

subsections.title: Bring List
subsections.body: Add items you need people to bring — drinks, dessert, chairs. Guests can claim what they're bringing so nothing gets doubled up.

subsections.title: Location
subsections.body: Add an address and it becomes a one-tap navigation link. Opens in Maps on any device.

subsections.title: Event Icons
subsections.body: Choose an icon to visually categorize your event — 🎂 birthday, ✈️ travel, 🏈 sports, and more. Icons are purely cosmetic — they help you scan your event list at a glance but don't change how events work.

sections.title: Occasions
sections.body: Occasions are things you celebrate. Annual, life-significant dates tied to people or moments.

subsections.title: Birthdays
subsections.body: Track birthdays for the people who matter. They surface on your Today tab on the day and in Beyond as they approach.

subsections.title: Anniversaries
subsections.body: Wedding anniversaries, work anniversaries, or any date worth marking each year. Optionally record the year it started.

subsections.title: Remembrance
subsections.body: Some dates are about honoring someone no longer here. Remembrance occasions don't require a Tether account — a name and a date is enough.

subsections.title: Create from an Occasion
subsections.body: Tap any occasion to create a related task or event — plan a birthday party, set a reminder to buy a gift, or invite the family to visit a grave. What you create is yours and not linked back to the occasion.

sections.title: Household
sections.body: Your household is the people you live with day to day. You have one household — it has many members. Everything shared in a household is automatic.

subsections.title: Shared by default
subsections.body: Tasks assigned to household members, events created within a household, and grocery lists are all visible to everyone in the house.

subsections.title: Joining a household
subsections.body: An admin invites you by email. You'll see a banner to accept or decline when you sign in. Joining is instant — no approval queue.

sections.title: Groups
sections.body: Groups are named collections of people — Benson Family, Book Club, Soccer Team. Think of them as smart distribution lists that know who's in Tether.

subsections.title: Invite in bulk
subsections.body: When creating an event, invite a whole group at once instead of adding people one by one. Individual group members can still be added separately.

subsections.title: Groups and Contacts
subsections.body: Household members and group members are automatically your contacts — no separate request needed. The spectrum from tight to loose: Household → Groups → Contacts.

sections.title: Contacts
sections.body: Contacts are your personal network in Tether. They can be invited to events, added to groups, and will eventually be linkable to occasions.

subsections.title: Adding contacts
subsections.body: Send a contact request by email. They accept or decline. Once connected, you can invite them to events and see shared occasions.

subsections.title: Privacy
subsections.body: Contacts only see what you share with them. Nothing is visible by default — you choose what to make household-visible, contact-visible, or private.
